deployment_question: How does the model perform on groups that were not represented during training?
definition: Partition data so all samples sharing a declared group identifier are held out together. This targets an operating point where within-group leakage is removed but between-group similarity may still vary.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: binary same-group similarity is forced to zero between train and test

implementation_recipe: Choose the grouping variable that would otherwise create leakage.
Assign whole groups, not individual samples, to folds.
Check label balance and group-size imbalance across folds.
quality_gates: Justify why the group variable corresponds to the deployment unit.
Measure residual similarity across held-out groups.
Report group counts, sample counts, and label distribution per fold.
failure_modes: Large groups can dominate a fold and produce unstable metrics.
Groups may be too coarse and confound the novelty axis with label prevalence.
Different groups can still be near-duplicates under another similarity definition.

citations.title: A Survey on Evaluation of Out-of-Distribution Generalization
citations.evidence: Surveys OOD evaluation protocols that hold out domains or groups to test generalization.

citations.title: Distributional bias compromises leave-one-out cross-validation
citations.evidence: Shows that leave-one-out style validation can be biased under distributional structure, motivating explicit group-aware checks.
